title: per-game lamp atlas
description: Human-authored lamp map for one game ROM — the lamp counterpart to ./names/<rom>.json (switches). Lives in the working directory by convention at ./lamps/<rom>.json (alongside ./orig, ./tables, ./names, ./sessions). Maps each PinMAME lamp number (a decimal string key, the same number ctx.get_lamp(n) / GetChangedLamps report) to a name or a richer descriptor. There is no mechanical extractor — lamp identities are reverse-engineered per table from the manual's lamp matrix plus the table .vbs and empirical observation; see the setup skill's 'Building the switch + lamp atlas' guidance. None ship with the plugin; create one per ROM as you pin lamps. This schema is informational — nothing validates against it at runtime.

oneOf.description: Shorthand: just the machine-friendly name for this lamp number (e.g. "mode_start").

label.description: Human label exactly as printed in the manual's lamp matrix (e.g. "MODE START", "WAR OF THE ENTS").

col.description: Lamp-matrix column (drive). Together with row, fixes the matrix cell the manual lists.

switch.description: Optional: the switch number whose shot lights/collects this lamp, when the lamp marks a shot (e.g. an Elf Ring shot lamp -> the ramp/orbit switch that scores it).

verified.description: true once the number has been confirmed empirically (lamp observed on in a known game state), as opposed to inferred from the manual matrix alone.
